title: Cotoneaster sternianus
intro: Cotoneaster sternianus, also known as Himalayan cotoneaster, is a deciduous shrub native to the Himalayas, characterized by its dark green leaves and showy white flowers. The health benefits of Cotoneaster sternianus are attributed to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Cotoneaster sternianus is used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and extracts, often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Cotoneaster sternianus is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This shrub is native to the mountainous regions of China and is a member of the large and diverse rose family. The family Rosaceae is further divided into several subfamilies, with Cotoneaster sternianus being a part of the subfamily Rosoideae. The subfamily Rosoideae also includes other rose-like plants, such as strawberries and raspberries. The classification of Cotoneaster sternianus reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the family Rosaceae.
